summary: Draft, closed-unmerged WIP on the trophy-list UI decomp. The main work rewrote large parts of src/melee/ty/tylist.c with typed TyListArg/TyListState access, added a C body for un_80313774, tightened tylist prototypes/structs, and made small formatting-only cast-spacing edits in gm/gr files. decomp.dev reported a net +792 matched bytes, including new 100% matches for un_8031263C and un_803124BC, but also several tylist regressions, which is important context because there was no PR body or human review feedback.

key_files.role: Primary rewrite of trophy list logic; added local archive/camera/display structs, implemented un_80313774, refactored scrolling, selection, text placement, camera setup, and JObj translation code.
Reshaped TyListArg and TyListState fields: links[3], jobjs[3], texts[3], typed TyListArg pointers, and signed scroll/cooldown fields.
Updated function prototypes to use TyListArg*/TyListState* directly and removed fn_80313BD8 from the public header after making it static in tylist.c.
Formatting-only compound literal spacing for GXColor casts.
Formatting-only function-pointer cast spacing in gmClassic_801B3500.
Formatting-only function-pointer cast spacing in grIceMt_801FA364.

what_changed: Added tylist-local helper structs TyListArchiveAnimData, TyListWaitData, TyListCameraState, TyListDispEntry, and TyListDispState to replace raw offsets around archive animation data, camera GObjs, display state, and wait data.
Added includes for sc/forward.h and baselib/aobj.h, consistent with the new Scissor use and direct HSD_AObjSetRate calls.

Changed un_803124BC and un_8031263C archive/symbol loading to use offsets from un_803FDD18, such as data + 0x608, data + 0x614, data + 0x9DC, data + 0xA58, instead of direct string literals like "TyDatai.dat" and "tyInitModelTbl".

Replaced a placeholder/nonmatched un_80313774 with C code that initializes TyListState, builds circular TyListArg links, creates list row JObjs/text objects, sets selection pointers, and calls un_80312BAC.
Refactored un_80312904 to take TyListArg* instead of void*, use arg->jobjs and arg->texts arrays, compute text positions from JObj translations, update colors, and populate trophy name/count text.
Refactored un_80312BAC, un_80312E88, un_8031305C, fn_80313BD8, fn_8031438C, un_8031457C, and un_803147C4 around typed state fields, array indexing, and clearer scroll/selection/camera logic.
Replaced large manual JObj translate/dirty-flag blocks in several places with HSD_JObjSetTranslateX, HSD_JObjSetTranslateY, and HSD_JObjSetTranslateZ.
Updated TyListArg layout from individual x0/x4/xC/x10/x14 fields and padding to links[3], jobjs[3], texts[3], x24, x25, idx, x28, x2C, and x30.
Updated TyListState field types for x270/x274/x278 to TyListArg*, made x29D and x2A0 signed s8, and retained unknown xNN-style names where semantics were not resolved.
Small unrelated style edits changed cast spacing to the project style `(GXColor) { ... }` and `(u8 (*)(...))` in gm_18A5.c, gmclassic.c, and gricemt.c.
smart_moves: Grouping parallel TyListArg fields into links[3], jobjs[3], and texts[3] matches the way the code iterates over neighboring entries, JObjs, and text slots, and makes ring-list traversal much easier to read.

Using existing HSD_JObjSetTranslate* helpers instead of open-coded translate assignments plus dirty-matrix flag logic reduced large repeated blocks while preserving the intended engine-side dirty behavior.
Referencing archive and symbol names through the existing un_803FDD18 string blob was a strong matching tactic for un_803124BC and un_8031263C; the bot reported both became 100% matches.
Making fn_80313BD8 static and tightening tylist.h prototypes reduced header surface area and replaced void* signatures with TyListArg*/TyListState* where the types were known.
The un_80313774 rewrite captured the list initialization pattern as circular links plus current/top/bottom selection pointers, which is reusable for other menu-list decomp work.
decomp_lessons: When a function loads many archive symbols, the compiler/data-layout match may require using offsets into an existing string table rather than new standalone C string literals.
For UI list structs, arrays are often better evidence than many separate xNN fields when the assembly indexes slots with a loop or fixed stride.
Prefer engine helper macros/functions such as GET_JOBJ and HSD_JObjSetTranslate* when they collapse expected assert/dirty-flag boilerplate and improve readability.
Signedness on small state bytes matters: this PR changed fields like x29D and x2A0 to s8 and removed many explicit casts around x29B/x29C/x29E/x29F-style UI state.
A net match gain can still hide risky regressions; this PR improved total matched code by +792 bytes but regressed several previously high-percentage tylist functions and was closed without merge.
Keep unknown fields as xNN/pad_NN until semantics are proven, but promote groups to named arrays or local structs when access patterns justify it.
naming_conventions: New local type names used the TyList prefix: TyListArchiveAnimData, TyListWaitData, TyListCameraState, TyListDispEntry, and TyListDispState.
TyListArg kept offset-based unknowns like x24, x25, x28, x2C, and x30, but promoted known groups to links, jobjs, and texts.
Function prototypes were tightened from void* or extra unused parameters to domain types, e.g. un_80312904(TyListArg* arg, s8 arg1) and un_8031305C(TyListState* state, s8 arg2).
Parameter names moved toward role names such as state, entry, arg, gobj, cam_desc, viewport, and scissor, while unresolved globals retained un_80xxxxxx names.
Formatting followed the style with a space after casts to compound literals and function-pointer casts: `(GXColor) { ... }`, `(u8 (*)(s32, s32, u8))`, and `(s32 (*)(HSD_GObj*, s32*))`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a local `char* data = un_803FDD18` and constant offsets for archive and symbol strings to reproduce existing data references.
Replace manual field-offset access with typed local structs only after the observed offsets are stable; examples include archive->jobjs[i], un_804A2D6C.x0/x4/x16, and disp->x140->x10.
Use literal float constants where the diff showed extern float symbols being removed, including values such as 6.5f, 0.41f, 0.028f, 0.029f, 1.0f, 0.0f, 999.0f, -0.8f, 0.8f, -0.6f, and 0.6f.
Model circular UI rows with predecessor/successor links and then advance via entry->links[0]/entry->links[1] or entry++ depending on the observed traversal.
Extracting un_80312E88_inline isolated the repeated JObj Y-translation update loop, though the bot still reported a small regression for un_80312E88.
HSD_AObjSetRate was changed to operate on anim_jobj->aobj with a literal 0.0f, replacing deeper raw object traversal in the old code.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice, and the PR body was empty.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code for GALE01 at 67.20%, a net +0.02% and +792 bytes.
decomp.dev reported 2 new matches in main/melee/ty/tylist: un_8031263C improved from 91.40% to 100.00%, and un_803124BC improved from 99.29% to 100.00%.
decomp.dev reported improvements for un_80313774 from 0.00% to 61.66%, .sdata2 from 12.90% to 91.23%, un_803147C4 from 78.58% to 85.97%, and un_80313508 from 98.71% to 99.29%.
decomp.dev also reported regressions for un_8031305C, fn_80313BD8, un_80312BAC, un_8031457C, un_80312904, .data, fn_8031438C, un_80312E88, and .sdata.
The PR was draft and closed unmerged, so future reuse should treat the code as a useful experiment rather than accepted final project state.
follow_up_queries: Find later accepted tylist PRs that reused TyListArg links/jobjs/texts from PR 2454.
Search for fixes to the PR 2454 regressions in un_8031305C, fn_80313BD8, un_80312BAC, and fn_8031438C.
Compare final tylist handling of un_803FDD18 string offsets against this PR's archive symbol loading changes.
Check whether HSD_JObjSetTranslate* replacements were kept in later matching work for un_80313508 and related functions.
Look for a merged PR that completes un_80313774 after this WIP reached 61.66%.
